3A_Bibliographic.citation: Nagata, J. M., Li, K., Sui, S. S., Talebloo, J., Otmar, C. D., Shao, I. Y., Kiss, O., Ganson, K. T., Testa, A., He, J., & Baker, F. C. (2025). Associations between media parenting practices and early adolescent consumption of R-rated movies and mature-rated video games. BMC Pediatrics, 25, 90.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: This study utilized data from 10,072 participants with follow-up data at Year 3 in the ABCD 5.1 release (2019-2021). From this Year 3 sample, one (1) individual without any R-rated movie consumption and mature-rated video game frequency data was excluded from the analytic sample. Additionally, individuals who did not have propensity scores were excluded (n=17), resulting in a final dataset of 10,054 adolescents.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Media parenting practices (6 categories: parental screen time modeling, mealtime screen use, bedroom screen use, use of screens to control behavior, parental monitoring of screen time, limiting screen time)
3C_Exposure.modalities: general screen use (parenting practices around screens)
3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous (mean scores on 4-point Likert scale from 1=Strongly Disagree to 4=Strongly Agree)
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: Parents completed a 14-item self-report questionnaire to assess their typical screen time practices with their children. Responses were rated on a 4-point Likert-type scale from 1 (Strongly Disagree) to 4 (Strongly Agree). Responses were aggregated into six categories of media parenting practices.
3C_Exposure.notes: Exposure is parenting practices around screen use, not child screen time itself.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Frequency of R-rated movie consumption and mature-rated video game use
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: Youth self-report items: frequency of R-rated movies and mature-rated video games (scale 0=Never to 3=All the time)
3D_Outcome.base_rate_if_binary: N/A - ordinal outcome treated as continuous
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: Adolescents self-reported their consumption of mature-content media using two questionnaire items. One item asked, 'How often do you watch R-rated movies?' and another asked, 'How often do you play mature-rated video games?' with responses on a scale from 0 (Never) to 3 (All the time).
3D_Outcome.notes: Outcome is mature content media consumption, not a standard health outcome.

3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex assigned at birth, race/ethnicity, parental education, household income, parental marital status, research site, COVID-19 pandemic timing
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: Separate ordinal logistic regression models were created for each of the six parent media monitoring behaviors and then used to evaluate the associations between each of those behaviors and the consumption of R-rated movies and mature-rated video games. Each model was adjusted for sociodemographic covariates.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Ordinal: Never to All the time
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Bedroom screen use: AOR 1.44 (1.36, 1.52) for mature video games

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Ordinal: Never to All the time
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Bedroom screen use: AOR 1.44 (1.36, 1.53) for R-rated movies

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Ordinal: Never to All the time
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Limiting screen time: AOR 0.72 (0.67, 0.77) for mature video games

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Ordinal: Never to All the time
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Limiting screen time: AOR 0.73 (0.68, 0.79) for R-rated movies

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Ordinal: Never to All the time
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: Parental monitoring: AOR 0.81 (0.77, 0.86) for mature video games

3G_Reporting_Quality.abstract_significance_verbatim: were positively associated... were significantly associated with
3G_Reporting_Quality.small_es_verbatim: it should be noted that all odds ratios (ORs), though significant, are relatively small

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: These findings can further inform guidelines such as those outlined by the American Academy of Pediatrics Family Media Use Plans.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The discussion interprets the cross-sectional associations between parenting practices and mature media consumption as supporting parental monitoring and limit-setting. It acknowledges ORs are small but recommends AAP Family Media Use Plans. The paper emphasizes that bedroom screen use and mealtime screen use are associated with more mature content consumption, while monitoring and limiting are protective.
